ksa_text: Ability to evaluate data sources for relevance, reliability, completeness, and objectivity to determine how source limitations affect analytical conclusions and data fitness.

dcwf.top_element_label: Identify functional- and security-related features to find opportunities for new capability development to exploit or mitigate vulnerabilities.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

top_anchors.element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology
Identify functional- and security-related features to find opportunities for new capability development to exploit or mitigate vulnerabilities.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DQ-A-002 (DQ, Ability) does not clear the moderate fit inclusion threshold of 0.43. Composite score 0.4159 is insufficient to establish relevance to quantitative analysis, statistical modeling, and financial analytics at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (ddat=0.416, onet=0.414, dcwf=0.399) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: DQ domain KSA DQ-A-002 excluded at composite 0.4159, but KSA text contains direct analytics-domain signals. Internal consistency: the presence of analytics terminology in a moderate fit KSA at score 0.4159 suggests the threshold is excluding genuinely relevant content.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Composite 0.4159 with confirmed analytics-domain terminology for a DQ (moderate fit) KSA indicates real role relevance for Quantitative Analyst. The 0.43 threshold is a calibration heuristic; text-signal confirmation overrides marginal score shortfall at this level.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude overturned. DQ KSA with composite 0.4159 and confirmed analytics-domain text signals included on internal consistency grounds.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does KSA DQ-A-002 (DQ, composite 0.4159) appear in published data analyst / BI role profiles and workforce frameworks?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: KSA content aligns with DCAT-AP, DAMA DMBOK data quality competency framework; DQ awareness is a documented expectation in senior IC analyst roles. Analytics-domain terminology in KSA text confirms practical relevance to IC ANL work. Composite 0.4159 with signal across ddat=0.416, onet=0.414, dcwf=0.399 is consistent with documented ANL competency expectations at IC-to-senior-IC level. External ground truth supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 include upheld. External evidence from DQ-domain frameworks confirms KSA relevance to Quantitative Analyst at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Single adversarial overturn by P3 internal consistency. Final decision: include. Composite 0.4159, domain DQ. Medium confidence — one agent challenged and overturned the preliminary assessment.
